china.description: 36氪获悉，近日，MOVA生态链企业奇点玩家（深圳）科技有限公司自研AI大模型产品“奇趣”完成千万级融资。本次融资将用于“奇趣”原生AI基座深化研究，以及“奇趣”大模型自身业务运营，团队扩张等需求。

china.title: 获韶音投资，一家AI公司做了“硬件版OpenClaw” | 36氪独家
china.description:
文｜邓咏仪
编辑｜苏建勋
当OpenClaw风暴席卷全球之时，无界方舟创始人曾晓东的反应不是兴奋，而是一种“终于被看见”的感觉。
“OpenClaw本质上是一个Agent框架，底层是一个会写代码的Agent，放在云端服务器或者电脑上。”他对《智能涌现》说，“我们的AIOS本质上也是一个Agent框架，但它是放在硬件端侧的OpenClaw——机器人、耳机、眼镜，都可以在这套OS上运行。”
无界方舟从

china.title: 36氪首发 | 00后交大博士做仿生飞行机器人，获启高、奇绩创坛、交大母基金等投资
china.description:
作者丨欧雪
编辑丨袁斯来
硬氪获悉，仿生扑翼飞行机器人公司——深圳鹰瞰智翼科技有限公司（下称“鹰瞰智翼”）近期已完成千万元级别天使轮融资，由启高资本领投，奇绩创坛及上海交大母基金跟投。资金将主要用于产品量产、下一代具身智能飞行机器人研发，以及全球首个机器人流体仿真引擎搭建。
鹰瞰智翼2025年3月成立于深圳，是全球第一家专注于具身智能扑翼机器人的公司。创始人陈昊是上海交通大学直博生，博士期间发表四

china.title: 抖音生活服务组织架构大调整，中小商家更重要了｜独家
china.description:
文｜李小霞
编辑｜乔芊 杨轩
36氪独家获悉，近期抖音生活服务完成了浦燕子接任后的第二次大的组织架构调整。
此次调整根据商户规模重新分为线上部门和线下部门。
月GMV5万以上的客户被放到线上部门，分为KA（月GMV10万以上）、自助（月GMV在5-10万）、酒旅三个版块。其中前二者又分别按行业细分为餐饮、到店综合。
月GMV5万以下的客户被放到线下部门。线下则按照区域划分为北区、南区、东区。相比此

papers.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and agents. Existing emotion-aware studies mainly treat em
papers.author: Moran Sun, Tianlin Li, Yuwei Zheng, Zhenhong Zhou, Aishan Liu, Xianglong Liu, Yang Liu

papers.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt changes. Existing single-agent strategies sample from on
papers.author: Yuxing Lu, Yushuhong Lin, Jason Zhang

papers.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well an agent invokes a tool) and intrinsic tool accurac
papers.author: Hy Dang, Quang Dao, Meng Jiang

papers.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware, role-orchestrated multi-agent LLM framework designed
papers.author: Ha Na Cho

papers.title: Human-in-the-Loop Control of Objective Drift in LLM-Assisted Computer Science Education
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00281v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in computer science education through AI-assisted programming tools, yet such workflows often exhibit objective drift, in which locally plausible outputs diverge from stated task specifications. E
papers.author: Mark Dranias, Adam Whitley

papers.title: Improvisational Games as a Benchmark for Social Intelligence of AI Agents: The Case of Connections
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00284v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We formally introduce a improvisational wordplay game called Connections to explore reasoning capabilities of AI agents. Playing Connections combines skills in knowledge retrieval, summarization and awareness of cognitive states of other agents. We sh
papers.author: Gaurav Rajesh Parikh, Angikar Ghosal

papers.title: Collaborative AI Agents and Critics for Fault Detection and Cause Analysis in Network Telemetry
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00319v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We develop algorithms for collaborative control of AI agents and critics in a multi-actor, multi-critic federated multi-agent system. Each AI agent and critic has access to classical machine learning or generative AI foundation models. The AI agents a
papers.author: Syed Eqbal Alam, Zhan Shu

papers.title: Signals: Trajectory Sampling and Triage for Agentic Interactions
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00356v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic applications based on large language models increasingly rely on multi-step interaction loops involving planning, action execution, and environment feedback. While such systems are now deployed at scale, improving them post-deployment remains
papers.author: Shuguang Chen, Adil Hafeez, Salman Paracha

papers.title: In harmony with gpt-oss
papers.description:
arXiv:2604.00362v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: No one has independently reproduced OpenAI's published scores for gpt-oss-20b with tools, because the original paper discloses neither the tools nor the agent harness. We reverse-engineered the model's in-distribution tools: when prompted without tool
papers.author: Borislav Mavrin

papers.title: Decision-Centric Design for LLM Systems
papers.description:

Abstract: LLM systems must make control decisions in addition to generating outputs: whether to answer, clarify, retrieve, call tools, repair, or escalate. In many current architectures, these decisions remain implicit within generation, entangling assessment a
